vBRIEFInfo.description: Mirror prd_render.py in spec_render.py so speckit narratives render in SPECIFICATION.md instead of being silently dropped.

plan.title: spec_render.py is item-centric and silently drops all speckit narratives

narratives.Problem: scripts/spec_render.py consumes only the Overview narrative plus plan.items, so any speckit-shaped spec (ProblemStatement, Goals, UserStories, Requirements, SuccessMetrics, EdgeCases, Architecture, TechDecisions, ImplementationPhases, PreImplementationGates) renders as a 44-byte H1-only file. prd_render.py was updated to the narrative-centric model; spec_render.py was not.
narratives.Action: Introduce SPECIFICATION_NARRATIVE_KEY_ORDER covering the interview/light and speckit key sets. Render present narratives in declared order, then remaining narrative keys alphabetically (matching prd_render behavior), then plan.items for hybrid/legacy specs. Keep Overview first. Verify behavior is flag-free for default speckit shape.
narratives.Test: Add tests/cli/test_spec_render.py (or extend existing) with a speckit-shaped fixture asserting the rendered SPECIFICATION.md contains ## ProblemStatement, ## Goals, ## Requirements headings and non-empty body content. Add a legacy interview-shaped fixture asserting Overview + items still render.
narratives.Outcome: task spec:render produces a multi-section SPECIFICATION.md for any speckit spec. 44-byte failure mode is eliminated. task check stays green.

items.title: Render narratives in declared order, then remaining alphabetically, then items
Add speckit-shaped fixture + assertion test in tests/cli/test_spec_render.py
Add legacy interview-shaped fixture regression test
Run task check and confirm green
